name: Psico Ayuda Venezuela
db_description:
# Red de Apoyo Psicológico Venezuela

Plataforma de apoyo psicológico para respuesta a emergencias en Venezuela. Conecta a personas que necesitan contención emocional con psicólogos verificados, y permite a los profesionales registrarse, subir sus credenciales y gestionar su disponibilidad.

**Producción:** [psicoayudaven.com](https://psicoayudaven.com)

## Características

- **Ruta de paciente** (`/ayuda`): acceso rápido a ayuda presencial o a distancia.
- **Directorio de profesionales** (`/ayuda/profesionales`): psicólogos verificados con estado en línea y contacto directo por WhatsApp. Los disponibles aparecen primero. Grilla de 2 columnas en escritorio.
- **Perfil profesional** (`/ayuda/profesionales/$id`): página pública por profesional con SEO (Open Graph, Twitter Cards, JSON-LD) y botón **Compartir** (Web Share API → portapapeles).
- **Registro de profesionales** (`/profesional/registro`): formulario en dos pasos. La credencial es un único número de colegiación agnóstico de país (+ colegio certificador opcional). Soporta psicólogos dentro y fuera de Venezuela.
- **Panel del profesional** (`/profesional/panel`): activar/desactivar disponibilidad ("En línea" / "No conectado").
- **Panel de administración** (`/admin`): revisar y verificar o rechazar registros. Acceso basado en base de datos (columna `user.role`).
- **Centro de cuenta** (`/cuenta`): hub según rol (login, panel, admin, cerrar sesión).
- **SEO** en todas las páginas públicas (títulos, descripciones, Open Graph, canonical vía `src/lib/seo.ts`).
- **Autenticación** con Better Auth (email + contraseña).
- UI _mobile-first_ con estética _liquid glass_ sobre la paleta Medicall. Navegación: barra inferior en móvil, barra superior en escritorio.

analysis.summary: psico-support (psicoayudaven.com) is a real, working psychological support directory platform built with TanStack Start (React SSR) deployed on Cloudflare Workers with D1 SQLite via Drizzle ORM. It provides a searchable, paginated directory of verified psychologists who can be contacted directly via WhatsApp, with a professional self-registration flow, admin approval workflow, and availability toggling. The codebase shows genuine, intentional design decisions documented throughout — this is not boilerplate. The domain psicoayudaven.com appears in the wrangler config, indicating real deployment intent. Created 2 days before the hackathon deadline with 24 commits and active schema iteration (7 migration files).
analysis.project_type: Full-stack SSR web app (TanStack Start) deployed to Cloudflare Workers edge runtime

architecture.pattern: SSR full-stack with co-located server functions (no separate API server)
architecture.orm_or_db_layer: Drizzle ORM over Cloudflare D1 (SQLite). DB connection is lazily initialised from the Cloudflare env binding set per-request via setCloudflareEnv(); a module-level cached singleton prevents repeated initialisation. Local dev uses better-sqlite3.
architecture.api_design: TanStack Start createServerFn RPCs — typed, co-located with route modules, validated with Zod. No REST/GraphQL surface. All auth-gated operations re-verify the session on every call via better-auth + DB role lookup.
architecture.separation_of_concerns: src/server/ holds all server-side logic (professionals.ts, locations.ts); src/db/ holds schema + connection; src/routes/ holds page components and their loaders/beforeLoad guards; src/lib/ holds cross-cutting utilities (auth, notifications, seo, env).
architecture.modularity: Good — shared WHERE builder (buildProfessionalWhere), shared insert builder (buildProValues), and shared Zod schemas prevent drift between list, random-pick, and registration paths.
notable_patterns: Admin role stored as DB column (not env list), looked up per-request — role changes take effect without redeploy
Paginated professional listing with shared WHERE clause reused by 'contact random' feature to guarantee consistent filter semantics
WhatsApp is the sole contact mechanism — no in-app messaging, no email blast
SSR loader pre-fetches first page then suspense query with refetchInterval:20s keeps availability status live
Population demographics stored as JSON text array with LIKE filter — acceptable for small table, noted in comments
Seven migration files showing active schema iteration (collapsed Venezuelan credential fields from 4 columns to 1 country-agnostic field)

code_organization.directory_structure: Clear: src/server/ (business logic), src/db/ (ORM), src/routes/ (file-based routing with TanStack), src/lib/ (utilities), src/components/ (UI). Drizzle migrations in drizzle/. Public assets in public/.
code_organization.naming_quality: Excellent — Spanish route paths (/ayuda, /profesional) match the target audience; English identifiers in code; Zod schemas named *Schema; server fns named after their action.
code_organization.documentation_quality: Unusually high for a hackathon — extensive 'ponytail:' inline comments on every non-obvious design decision explaining the why, not just the what. AGENTS.md and docs/ui-style.md present.
code_organization.reasoning: Code quality significantly exceeds typical hackathon submissions. Intentional tradeoffs are explicitly documented (e.g., ORDER BY RANDOM() acceptable while <1k rows, LIKE filter can't use index). Consistent TypeScript throughout with Zod validation on all server function inputs.

production_readiness.secrets_handling: BETTER_AUTH_SECRET in .env.example (not hardcoded). D1 binding and database_id committed to wrangler.jsonc (D1 IDs are not secrets, but the database_name and ID are public). Sentry DSN is optional via VITE_SENTRY_DSN.
production_readiness.security_notes: Admin operations re-check DB role on every server fn call — no stale JWT privilege escalation. Public listing only returns verified professionals. WhatsApp numbers are publicly visible in the directory listing with no auth wall — any scraper can harvest them. No rate limiting on any endpoint. Email verification is not enforced before professional registration (account creation immediately creates a 'pending' row that admins must approve — this is intentional but means anyone can occupy the pending queue).
production_readiness.scalability_notes: Cloudflare Workers + D1 is edge-distributed and auto-scales. D1 is eventually consistent on replicas but reads on Workers are handled by the primary unless using read replicas. LIKE '%...%' filters and ORDER BY RANDOM() are fine at hackathon scale (<1k rows) but would need indexing strategy if the directory grows to tens of thousands.
production_readiness.reasoning: Strong fundamentals — auth, role-based access, Sentry integration, Zod validation, friendly error messages that never leak raw SQL. Missing: no CI pipeline, no tests, no rate limiting, and sendDefaultPii:true in Sentry logs user IPs (potential privacy concern for vulnerable earthquake victims).

maturity.is_real_or_boilerplate: Real — seven DB migrations show active schema evolution over the project's two-day life (collapsed Venezuelan credential fields, added country/estado/ciudad, added role column). The wrangler.jsonc contains an actual D1 database_id and custom domain (psicoayudaven.com). Server functions have production-quality error handling and user-facing Spanish messages.
maturity.reasoning: Clearly a real working MVP, not scaffolding. However, 24 commits, 1 contributor, no tests, and no CI pipeline are significant gaps for a system handling vulnerable users' contact information. The vitest dependency is declared but no test files exist.

viability.reasoning: Strong viability: the core user flow (find a verified psychologist by location/modality, tap to open WhatsApp) is fully implemented and deployable to Cloudflare today. The admin moderation workflow is complete. The WhatsApp-as-contact-layer is pragmatic for Venezuela's connectivity constraints. The main gap is building the professional supply side — the platform is useless without a critical mass of registered, admin-approved psychologists, and there is no outreach or seeding mechanism in the codebase.

key_features: Searchable, paginated directory of verified psychologists filterable by modality (in-person/remote), Venezuelan state, city, and population demographic
Professional self-registration with two-step form (account creation + credential details)
Admin approval panel: review pending professionals, approve/reject, promote users to admin role
'Contact random professional' button — picks from the same filtered pool as the current list view and opens WhatsApp
Per-professional public profile pages with SSR SEO head tags
PWA support (service worker, manifest, maskable icons)
Role-based access: admin role stored in DB, checked per-request without redeploy
analysis.merge_potential: Low — this is a standalone, domain-specific mental health professional directory. It does not expose data APIs or webhooks that other projects could consume. Could theoretically link into a broader aid portal but would require significant integration work.
red_flags: No tests despite vitest being configured — zero confidence in regression safety
No CI/CD pipeline — deployments are manual `npm run build && wrangler deploy`
WhatsApp numbers are publicly accessible in the directory with no authentication wall — creates a scraping/spam vector targeting professionals
No rate limiting on any server function — professional registration, random-pick, and listing are all unthrottled
Sentry configured with sendDefaultPii:true — logs IP addresses of users who may be earthquake victims seeking mental health help (privacy risk)
Email verification is not enforced — professionals can register and occupy the admin queue with unverified emails
Single contributor with 24 commits over 2 days — bus factor of 1
i18n messages exist only in de.json and en.json, but the app UI is entirely in Spanish — the i18n infrastructure appears unused
